summary: The main claim is: This post summarizes my understanding of what the Löbian Obstacle is (having re-read the paper,) and why I think it is still an important idea one decade after its publication.
If we can't reliably thwart the Löbian Obstacle, we should consider alternatives: Can we reliably attain high certainty informal guarantees of alignment for arbitrarily deep simulacra hierarchies?
The main claim is: I also do not mean that we should have been actively doing these things before it was culturally relevant.
Didn't we understand how those systems function in the real world?

The main claim is: Some announcements: There’s a brief survey of my audience you can take here - filling it out will help me get a sense of where you all are at career-wise and how the podcast would better serve you.
The AXRP store is closing in a week - so if you want to buy merchandise, now’s your last chance to do so!

The main claim is: I believe that we should stay away from AI systems that look like and behave like humans because they could become rogue AIs and because they could fool us and influence us (to advance their interest or someone else’s interests, not ours).
The AI systems that are more powerful will be selected and the recipe shared with other humans.

The main claim is: After my previous post on the marginal existential risk from AI in world where nuclear war is likely, here I review some canonical AI risk literature, describe my assessment on the current level of AI risk, and conclude that an "AI pause"…
The main claim is: "Conditioning" as in, placing your reward model's reward in the prompt.
There is a couple of discussions regarding conditioning / decision transformer training.
The main claim is: I knew that the responses were generated by a NN, and that you could even ask Bing to adjust the responses.
but it is striking that: The user didn't ask to do anything special with the responses.

top_comment.author: Charlie Steiner
top_comment.text: My guess is this is a bug, and that normally the suggested responses come from using the LLM to predict what the user's side of the conversation will say, but maybe because of the LLM's response getting interrupted by an automated filter, the suggested responses were generated by using the LLM to predict Bing chat's side of the conversation.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: My guess is this is a bug, and that normally the suggested responses come from using the LLM to predict what the user's side of the conversation will say, but maybe because of the LLM's response getting interrupted by an automated filter,…

summary: The main claim is: + How are you going to solve all that distributed complexity – all those principal-agent problems expressing themselves in many different ways for many different humans living in many different circumstances – with one general solution?
To me that does not clarify how adding yet another but much more powerful and much more alien agent in the mix is going to help solve all those other principal-agent problems in the mix.
The main claim is: Dim 138 is higher for tokens with short embedding vectors.
Note that the entries in W^T W are the dot products of the embedding vectors of tokens.
The main claim is: In particular, I believe that the "cultural intelligence hypothesis" explains to a significant extent the origins of human intelligence.
Chimps’ lack of cultural learning/language use skills and lack of access to cultural data reinforced each other.
The main claim is: • We explore a paradigm that adds physics simulation to the LM reasoning pipeline (§3) to make the reasoning grounded within the physical world.
The main claim is: We’re going to be hosting Stuart Russell, as well as several other AI safety researchers, to speak about the field.
ALTER, with the University of Haifa and the Technion, is excited to announce the details about its upcoming conference introducing AI safety in Israel.

The main claim is: One reason I think this hasn't happened is that few people feel qualified to do this (this isn't the main reason-- I think the main reason is that people are too busy, and there's a lot that needs to be done).
The main claim is: I'm thinking something that you add your API keys, and it has a pleasant interface for writing prompts, seeing results, and storing all the prompts and outputs for your reference.
Maybe integrating with more general tools for thought.

The main claim is: This is the de-facto mechanism by which most theoretical work on alignment avoids advancing capabilities most of the time, and I think it is the main mechanism by which most theoretical work on alignment should aim to avoid advancing capab…

But for most theoretical alignment work most of the time, where there are some capabilities implications but they’re not very direct or immediately dangerous on their own, I think “nobody cares” is the right privacy model under which to op…
The main claim is: We see the Inverse Scaling Prize as a step in the direction of catching more outer alignment failures.
What data is most effective for exposing inverse scaling laws?
